1. ^ In a Festive run, upon learning about another NPC race's significant holidays you must celebrate them as well. 2. ^ You must celebrate every holiday you know of. 3. ^ Stoneblock leaves an area of air around you proportional to your island's size. 4. ^ There is no ambient lighting in Stoneblock. 5. ^ Monogamy only 6. ^ The schedule book seems to have infinite pages that can be looked through at any time. The pages cannot be torn out. The pen will not run out of ink, but only when used with the book. If you lose these items, they will be returned to you the next time you wake from sleeping. You can remove penalties for scheduling if you cross out the offending item and write the correct activity. Activities must be specific enough to allow you to recall the events of the day with a hypothetical clarity of 3.5 on the Lucet scale. 7. ^ All ensouled beings have access to cheats. 8. ^ Women may be of any ensouled (non-fractionally) race. 9. ^ If May the Best Racer Win is taken alongside Vengeance, the World Dragon's son will challenge you to a Go-Kart race instead of trying to kill you, though he may still attack if sufficently angered. 10. ^ The World Dragon will try to win the Go-Kart race using any method necessary, including cheating. 11. ^ Your alignment begins as Neutral, and will remain that way as long as the sum of Deeds in your Moral Ledger remains within a threshold of one murder or 0.8 rapes caused/prevented. Defeating The World Dragon saves millions of lives. 12. ^ The death barrier is invisible. 13. ^ The death barrier only affects you. 14. ^ Linked is incompatible with any Run that allows the run to extend past the death of the World Dragon. It's technically compatible with Runs that let you win without killing the World Dragon but what's the point of it, then? Are you even taking this seriously? 15. ^ Monsters with regeneration abilities may ignore storm damage. 16. ^ Friends in *-Op runs cannot choose their own items. 17. ^ Specifically, the five Major Bosses. This does not include the Multiplicity Demon, nor does it include the Bonus Demon. 18. ^ Friends in The Real Treasure must be real friends, not just Facebook friends. 19. ^ All Life is Precious only counts macroscopic creatures larger than 3 cm (in some direction). 20. ^ Space is divided up into cubes sized 0.21357 meters3, which are called Skal. Buying, selling, leasing, lending, and gifting of owned Skal is done through a formless HUD visible only to the individual navigating it known as the 'Verse Domain Authority ('VDA). Unclaimed Skal can be purchased by ensouled beings if they have passed through them in the last 5 minutes. Every Skal costs 1 gem and must be renewed for 1 gem each η-batch (1070.064 hours).

Domain Owners (DOs) may place a set of Owner's Rules (ORs) which applies to all of the Skal in their Domain, whether or not they own it, are lent it, or are leasing it. Each new OR requires an upfront η-batchly payment of (1 + num previous ORs)2 gems.

All ORs are made up of 3 Rulewords preceded by an implicit "NO". It is not always clear what the rules mean on first inspection, but many guides exist which document the most useful, common, or unintuitive ones.
These are the 103 Rulewords:
None, all, self, other, owner, red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, violet, pink, black, white, brown, gray, invisible, magic, normal, even, odd, spotted, striped, random, ordered, read, question, talk, shout, wear, look, hold, equip, change, dance, walk, run, jump, conceal, eat, drink, attack, defend, shoot, throw, worship, summon, take, grab, mine, burrow, shirt, pants, shoes, robe, clothes, book, tablet, dish, bowl, tissue, sword, shield, bow, wand, stick, sharp, dull, machine, metal, wood, flesh, fabric, tissue, string, plastic, glass, stone, ore, gems, flower, plant, animal, shiny, face, god, angel, demon, worm, dragon, man, woman, child, animal, thing, spirit, monster, nonbeing, love, hate, big, tiny.

Here are some possible ORs and their interpretations:
NO LOOK SHINY GLASS => No looking in mirrors
NO TINY INDIGO MAGIC => Minor psyonics are forbidden
NO GREEN TINY HATE => Goblins must be respected
NO TINY GREEN HATE => No outwardly envious actions
NO LOVE DANCE DRAGON => Dragons may not be penetratee
NO WEAR CLOTHES ALL => You must be naked
NO SELF ATTACK SELF => No self harm
NO SELF EQUIP NONBEING => No suicide

ORs may not cause instant death, nor may they cause instant bodily, spiritual, or magical harm. ORs can never affect thoughts (unless projected psionically) or purely internal actions. The 'VDA will warn ensouled beings of new ORs when entering Domains. There is a 5 second grace period to stop all offending actions (which will not be able to be completed) before severe pain and loss of bodily/spiritual control forcibly ends offending actions.

ORs cannot defy the laws of physics nor affect objects caused by actions from outside their Domain (eg, a bullet from outside a domain cannot be stopped by an OR).

The 'VDA displays the boundaries of all Domains, the ORs active within, and Truenames of the DO and any Domain Tenants (DTs) or Domain Lendees (DLs).

Since Skal may be owned, lent, or leased, the general term for the owner, lender, or leaser of Skal is Domain Nominator (DN).

Domain Lending allows DNs to retain ownership of several Skal while designating a DL who can place new ORs within that Skal or who is recognized as a DN for the sake of some ORs. Domain Lending does not require the DL's consent and may be revoked at any time. If the DN is not allowed to create new ORs, neither will any DLs or further dependents.

Domain Leasing requires an agreement between DN and DT: For a negotiated number of η-batches, the Skal within the leasing contract is lent to the DT. This contract cannot be dissolved by either party early (except through death). Whether or not DTs can apply their own ORs to the leased Skal is up to the contract.

DTs and DLs may lease and lend the Skal they are DT or DL to. Revocation of Skal which leased or lent to a DN who has further leased or lended that Skal will cancel all lendings and leasing contracts dependent upon that Skal

Gifting Skal transfers ownership completely with no obligation to sustain any ORs. All Domain Leases and Domain Lends will stay in effect after gifting, but as before the new DO may cancel Domain Lends at any time.
Death of a DO immediately frees all Skal they own. Any lent or leased Skal have their ownership transferred to the highest DN in the Domain Dependency Linking Graph (DDLG). Death of DTs or DLs will remove them from the DDLG and affix all outgoing edges to the DN above them.

Any given Skal can only be owned by one ensouled being at a time. The same Skal cannot be lent or leased to multiple ensouled beings who would be siblings in the DDLG.

An η-batch is the amount of time it takes for a Weft Glyder to completely mature from its larval stage. This is an extremely stable measure and, despite the dangers, some more primitive civilizations still breed large amounts of Weft Glyders just to keep the time. 21. ^ Magical Midas Lacquer is just gold paint. 22. ^ You will receive shipments of Magical Midas Lacquer once a month via magic portal. Gefelt will know if you tamper with his product. 23. ^ You cannot buy the Magical Midas Lacquer yourself. Someone else must buy it. Co-op players or any allies also cannot buy it off you. 24. ^ You can take the trash can with you. 25. ^ You get to spend all your points before your evil clone/doppelganger does, and if you pick an option that spawns a unique character they will be unable to pick that option. As long as you kill the World Dragon you technically don't have to kill your evil clone/doppelganger to win but it's still probably a good idea to get rid of that guy anyways. If taken with Vengeance, your evil clone/doppelganger and the World Dragon's son may work together if they meet at an opportune time. 26. ^ Your evil clone/doppelganger's name is your own name reversed. 27. ^ You can take your items and powers with you. If the human race goes extinct while you're in The 'Verse the run is considered lost. 28. ^ You will still experience Truedeath after, but not before being beaten by your mother. 29. ^ If your mother is dead, she is temporarily resurrected to beat you. 30. ^ Dog Material is identical to Star Material except for its dog-like qualities. Dogscape turns Earth Constructs into Dog Constructs and Metal Constructs into Dogmetal Constructs. Pre-existing Dog Constructs and Dogmetal Constructs are not affected. While Dogscape is active, Elves love dogs and Dwarves hate them. Dog-eating cultures are not reviled for doing so while Dogscape is active. The broodmother is always watching. 31. ^ The Multiplicity Demon can spread to the offspring of anything possessed by it, even if only one parent is possessed. It can only possess ensouled beings. 32. ^ You lose your shards when bosses respawn. 33. ^ Any items that don't spawn on your person will not be in the cage with you. 34. ^ Stopping time with Time Out affects you as well, including brain activity. Time Out does not come with the ability to unstop time. 35. ^ The moon takes 7 days to make a full revolution 36. ^ Thank you to KnowYourMeme for providing reference for several items. 37. ^ The acupuncture needles will never be in a spot that is immediately fatal or disabling like your eyes or neck. 38. ^ The Elfkin tribe in Inconsequential Land Dispute scales with the starting size of your island and all the land that belongs to it. By default, it's a town of about 50 Elfkin. If you take The Wanderer, it becomes a nation of 6 million Elfkin. 39. ^ Cannot write the paper itself. Works for others as well 40. ^ if the paper is stolen it no longer works, meaning you can't put it inside or attach it to something that's written on it and expect it to come back 41. ^ You will not age during your time working your fast food service job and will be provided meals. You are transported to a restaurant pocket dimension while working and time will be frozen within The 'Verse. Whatever is on your person will be transported with you both ways. 42. ^ Requires no power or film. 43. ^ Stops working after the 40th photo is printed. 44. ^ Comes with a free lanyard. 45. ^ You will seriously never need to take this many photos. To put it into perspective: if you took 1 photo every second, it would take 50,000,000 seconds for you to run out of photos. 46. ^ Artificial souls can be combined to created more powerful artificial souls which can animate larger amounts of matter. 47. ^ You can still think and become unplastic while solid plastic. 48. ^ You can freeze/unfreeze from this state at any time. 49. ^ You do not need to eat, drink, or breathe and will not age. 50. ^ Succubi won't obey you unless you can strike a deal with or manipulate them somehow 51. ^ The Isles of Albion are populated by elfs, dwarfs, blackbloods, green elfs, obdurons, and have a terrible infestation of mud slimes. 52. ^ While fasting you may drink water, coffee, urine, elfengetränk, or tea without breaking your fast. 53. ^ Supplements which don't contain calories can be consumed. 54. ^ Potions cannot be consumed. 55. ^ Correction: Daily dose is not actually infinite. It's only a single bottle. Sorry. 56. ^ Unholy aligned races will "get" the reference ;) 57. ^ Iron nails are size 30D. 58. ^ the fern on your island, if taken, is Polypodium glycyrrhiza 59. ^ Light absorption bonuses stack multiplicatively with other sources such as melanation. 60. ^ Each novelty keychain must have a volume less than or equal to 0.005m³ 61. ^ The scroll of genocide has a 25% chance to be cursed. Dragons, liches, and angels are ungenocidable. 62. ^ The kind hispanic man needs to be supplied with a lawnmower. He is incapable of anything other than mowing. 63. ^ Lambda Calculus will occur even if you die and are resurrected before passing the stone. Your dead body will have another stone within its urinary tract which can be retrieved. Bringing both stones to the World Dragon will unlock New Game++ and Theater Mode. 64. ^ The restaurant from Comfort Food will always be 1,000 square meters, no matter what restaurant is picked. 65. ^ Binary Tree replaces your starting tree 66. ^ They have no way off the island and are expecting you to provide some kind of transport. They will still spawn on your island even if you don't start there. 67. ^ You can choose any of these stats to be affected by Stonks: Spd, Chr, Dex, Str, End, Int, Fth, or Ach. 68. ^ You must be the CEO or on the board of directors for a company you started to count as "yours" 69. ^ If your company is acquired by a larger one or involved in a merger, then the stat multiplier is set to 1 and the company is no longer considered "yours". 70. ^ The curve governing the multiplier for your chosen stat is: { 0 <= x < 1 : stat = stat * x, 1 < x : stat = stat * (ln(x) + 1) }. 71. ^ If you go bankrupt, the stat that company represented has a permanent 1/8th reduction from its base before the multiplier. 72. ^ You can only have up to 9 companies that are actively considered "yours" at a time. New companies started after the 9 company limit cannot be "switched to" unless another company is disbanded, goes bankrupt, or is acquired/merged into another company. 73. ^ If your company disbands, you do not keep the stat multiplier (unless it's <1). 74. ^ Wraps around back to A after Z 75. ^ does not actually affect your books' sales 76. ^ Learned Sage multiplies your Int by MAX(1.05, 1 + 0.005*num of video game related items you're wearing) 77. ^ All monster pets hatched from eggs have a power level of 1. 78. ^ The air is only edible while sandwhiched between the buns and entering your mouth. Once put in your mouth they lose their properties. 79. ^ Consuming more than 8 chatterberries within an hour can cause severe bouts of glossolalia which may last for up to 4 hours. 80. ^ The titan has no resources on his island has no qualms about taking yours. 81. ^ Macs are considered analogous to PCs for the sake of this item 82. ^ You have to pay for the Fast Food you order using Remember What They Took From You (adjusted for inflation) 83. ^ Read each capitalized letter out loud. 84. ^ The murder was on Earth, not in The 'Verse. 85. ^ You are not safe from S-Risks. 86. ^ Hellhounds are hostile to non-demonic creatures. 87. ^ Reaching level 999 while swimming in water nonstop takes approximately 50 years. 88. ^ If necessary, any relevant artists will be held static in a Preservation Room in the Realm of the Conserved for the duration of your challenge 89. ^ Your password must include at least one: uppercase letter, ASCII symbol, and digit. 90. ^ The prefixes from Insane Pulls are only cosmetic 91. ^ Consider the consequences of choosing this carefully. 92. ^ Coin-Op players affected by We Need To Cook will require currency to be either freshly minted or traded for. 93. ^ Please see the footnote for the "smoking hot" babe. 94. ^ Asbestos Removal Service does not remove asbestos placed into the island after the run begins. Your island may or may not have been built using any asbestos at all. 95. ^ Goons disappear after 1 week. 96. ^ Attempting to kill or threaten The Points Dealer will cause him to disappear along with all his points and anything you've used previously to trade with him. 97. ^ The base rate of growth is additively increased by log7(x0.75 + 1), where x is the number of months. 98. ^ The base rate of growth is additively increased by log7(x + 1), where x is the number of months. 99. ^ The offspring of ensouled and unsouled beings will possess a fractional half-soul. 100. ^ Fractional souls will mix as expected in further offspring. 101. ^ Supermodel does not affect your overall weight. Mass is redistributed elsewhere roughly as you would expect. 102. ^ NPCs are unaware of this condition and don't seem to pick up on the obvious visual cues. 103. ^ Creatures can change their alignments, but it will be followed by obvious changes in their appearance. 104. ^ You will not remember taking this item nor its addon, and neither will appear on the Item Receipt item. 105. ^ Only living creatures can be duplicated. Spawned duplicates do not have souls but are otherwise identical. The creature must remain alive inside the spawner for the entire interval. The mass used to determine the length of an interval is the maximum mass of the creature during that interval. 106. ^ any upgrades to your speed no longer apply, but you can still gain more 107. ^ Violating any of these rules (or those of the addons) will make you wanted for the highest possible crimes in every single society. 108. ^ The mini golf course is supported by Star Material 109. ^ The skinwalker will try its best to mimick their voice, but it doesn't actually understand human language. 110. ^ The skinwalker is very hungry. 111. ^ The skinwalker will always have 99 kilograms of mass, so its imitation of the chosen person may have some slight distortions if they would take less/more mass to imitate. 112. ^ Coconut oil is fractionated, but unrefined. 113. ^ You must talk for at least 5 minutes about TV Tropes. Failure to do so will remove your ability to move permanently. 114. ^ Some immunity dogs can prevent your mother's death but generally not retroactively, refer to the dog in question for details. 115. ^ Achievements increase your Score. 116. ^ The man on who calls you on the tablet is named is Rob Thommarson. He lives in Hackensack, New Jersey. 117. ^ The tablet has no other functionality besides producing power and receiving Rob's video calls 118. ^ the fence on the deck is wood and a meter high 119. ^ The Curtain Of Hlidskjalf restores knowledge as soon as the object is uncovered. 120. ^ Knowledge erasure affects all beings and their perceptions of anything which references whatever is enclosed by it. 121. ^ It is fireproof and very durable, but can be destroyed. 122. ^ Destroying the Curtain of Hlidskjalf will uncover the Highest and Most Primal Secret. 123. ^ An ingrown hair is a hair with a curled shaft that forms a closed loop while the hair is entirely covered by one or more layers of skin. 124. ^ "near" = 10-50km away 125. ^ This will not help you learn magic. 126. ^ This will also not help you learn magic. 127. ^ The bridges from Yoku Blocks are made of Void Material, which can only be moved using Condensed Soul equipment 128. ^ Special thanks to Spotify® Technology S.A. and FanDuel Sports Network 129. ^ This affects all NPCs, regardless of their alignments, culture, relationship with you, current emotional state, and social status. 130. ^ Holy books and pamphlets may be from Earth or The 'Verse. 131. ^ If they win their run, you will return to Earth alongside them. 132. ^ If they lose, you will die of natural causes. 133. ^ You will not age, hunger, or thirst within the confines of this room. 134. ^ It is 1.5 meters tall. 135. ^ Never ending tissue box has a daily limit of 10 tissues 136. ^ your long lost brother can never be a frigid dwarf 137. ^ The goat is pregnant with demonspawn. 138. ^ The cannellini beans have added salt 139. ^ animal species are selected such that they cannot interbreed 140. ^ 141. ^ the glow-in-the-dark effect works like photoluminescent paint. 142. ^ You only look superficially like trollface/trollge. You do not gain his powers. 143. ^ You can also see perfectly out of your sunglasses during the day. 144. ^ Those who ingest the effluence of Collin's Key speak of its virtues: a sumptuous and pleasing taste, almost too rich; a quickening of the faculties; and a sudden impartment of frenetic vigour. Those who have never tasted say it is best never to. The signs of the Key are many: a smile too wide, laughter too bright and too clear, talk of events as "challenges", and eyes which seem to look past the world. Some say the key was created by two mighty demons who sup only upon dragon's blood. Some say the Key unlocks the human mind. 145. ^ The Sculptor does not apply to Fell Gargoyles 146. ^ Reliving the life of abyssal creatures may have unintended consequences. 147. ^ The tablet has an image viewer and can save up to 15mb of images. It comes preloaded with a single image of a smug-looking elf character from a popular fictional series often posted about on these kinds of sites. 148. ^ Your speech and writing is considered to be UTF-8 and any utterances will be mapped to the closest matching symbol. 149. ^ 150. ^ The rate of growth is additively increased by log_16(1 + x), where x is the total number of thumbs fed to your tree. 151. ^ This item will always take effect as soon as you enter The 'Verse. It will not retroactively change the biology of beings within The 'Verse. 152. ^ On Hard Mode Snoopy is in his pilot form and must be defeated in battle before joining the island 153. ^ Updates to stats are shared between Mini Me or Personal Army clones instantly. 154. ^ If your Mini Me clone dies, you cannot merge back with it. 155. ^ You experience the perceptions of all of your Mini Me/Personal Army clones at once and your Int is unaffected by stat halving. 156. ^ Conspiracies cannot affect The 'Verse or any of its denizens. 157. ^ the bell from Ring for Service is made of bell bronze 158. ^ the length of the rope on the tire swing scales with the size of the tree 159. ^ MikePlays doesn't seem to be a twitch stream, he doesn't seem to address anyone in the chat and he's not aware he's being recorded. He rarely speaks, mostly only uttering obscenities when he's losing in a game. 160. ^ Can create portals on any surface. 161. ^ The 808 grains have been denatured by Aetherial radiation. 162. ^ Must not be wearing shoes 163. ^ Killing any monsters during your islandversary party will turn them all hostile again. 164. ^ *Does not include Abyssal Fiends 165. ^ Lem is a game for 1-17 players. The Lem Archive Deck contains 72 cards composed of five Suits (11 Star, 23 Echo, 6 Cipher, 17 Anomaly, and 15 Origin), while the Lem Refraction Deck contains the same composition of 72 cards (albeit without sigils) but is not present at the start of play. The Archive Deck begins reverse-face upward as the Draw Stack. The Discard Queue begins empty. The Skyline is created by revealing exactly three cards obverse. Each player receives 4 starting cards into each of their Left Hand and Right Hand in reverse-face upward posture. Cards in either Hand may be held at any physical angle, provided they are not rotated in a way that would reveal obverse to others. When the physical grip of a card rests in a position such that its held area reasonably borders more than one Hand's spatial boundary, that same physically held card may, be evaluated as belonging to each such Hand simultaneously. All face exposure must follow legal posture change. Rotation is permitted and meaningful only for interpretive comparisons. In all cases, if rotation affects eligibility, the angle at the moment of comparison is binding and cannot be retroactively altered.

Turn order proceeds clockwise. First, every Left Hand takes its turn in order. Then, every Right Hand takes its turn in the same order. This completes one rotation. A turn consists of exactly two Actions. The three legal Action types are draw, discard, and play-to-Skyline. A turn may perform any combination of these Actions, provided they are legal. A card drawn must be taken from the top of the Draw Stack unless it is empty, in which case it must be taken from the front of the Discard Queue. A card discarded enters the rear of the Discard Queue. Cards may not be discarded anywhere else unless legally undiscarded. A card played to the Skyline enters obverse. A card leaving the Skyline maintains its rotational posture.

A reverse-face card is Unobserved. Its Suit and Degree cannot be referenced for rules unless it becomes Observed. A card becomes Observed when it is placed obverse through a legal Action or legal flip. A card that is Observed cannot be reversed to Unobserved unless the Skyline contains exactly three Observed cards whose Suits correspond to the necessary sigil alignment. This alignment depends both on Suit identity and rotational comparison at the moment of the reversal declaration. If any card in Skyline alters its angle prior to resolution of that reversal, the reversal becomes illegal and does not resolve. A reversal cannot be pre-declared. It must be declared at the moment posture is changed.

A card may be flipped up to 2 times in a single Action. The intermediate posture must be legal for the zone that card occupies during that moment. Only the final posture is binding on state evaluation. If flip changes rotation, the new angle becomes the new reference orientation and prior angle no longer exists for rules evaluation except when used to retroactively invalidate outcomes, described later.

Star cards entering Skyline force the next draw for that same Hand to be taken from the Discard Queue if the Discard Queue is not empty. Echo cards entering a Hand cause all Unobserved cards already in that Hand to become connected. Connected cards cannot be separated for draw or discard. If a Hand discards a connected card, the entire connected set must be discarded. Cipher cards Observed anywhere are counted globally. If the count equals the number of players currently in the rotation, the next Action of the Hand whose Action triggered that equality must be a discard if legal. Anomaly cards Observed in Skyline prevent all reversals of posture until no Anomaly remains Observed in Skyline. Origin cards Observed in Skyline force the next three discard Actions of that same Hand to place cards at the bottom of the Discard Queue instead of the rear.

Degree exists only for Star, Echo, and Cipher cards. Star cards are Degree 1, Echo cards are Degree 2, Cipher cards are Degree 3. These Degrees govern interaction priority: Degree 1 forbids this same Hand from drawing twice in a rotation, Degree 2 permits a draw even if a discard has already occurred in this turn, and Degree 3 cannot remain in a Hand once Observed and must be immediately placed to Skyline upon becoming visible.

Cards in either Hand may form a sequence if all cards in that set share a strictly monotonic Degree progression when read in Hand order by their present rotational direction. These sequences must remain physically adjacent in the Hand. If any card in the sequence rotates to an angle that breaks the monotonic order, the entire sequence dissolves. A sequence cannot leave a Hand until it is broken OR until every card in the sequence has been flipped at least once.

During the post-Action phase a non-Echo card from the Discard Queue can be undiscarded and placed reverse-face upward into the current Hand, but only if that exact same card, in the same orientation and with identical rotation, could have been legally discarded by this same Hand in the immediately preceding Action sequence. Undiscard occupies one Action slot but is neither draw nor discard for purposes of Star/Echo/Cipher interactions.

If a card which has been undiscarded can, by angle or Suit-match under current Table State, be determined to have been a valid candidate for belonging to a monotonic sequence in that Hand in any earlier turn of this round, then every Action performed by that Hand since the earliest such turn is immediately re-evaluated. Any Action from that window that would have been illegal under the hypothetical presence of that card inside that potential sequence is retroactively invalidated and every Action in the chain since that turn must be unwound in reverse order. Cards returned by unwinding must adopt their current observed/unobserved posture and current angle. If unwinding causes a further violation, unwinding continues recursively until a stable Table State exists.

The Refraction Deck remains inactive until either the Draw Stack is empty, or a reverse-face Archive card is placed in a zone that normally requires obverse. Once active, the Refraction Deck forms its own Draw Stack and Discard Queue and follows all rules above. Cross movement between Archive and Refraction is only permitted when forbidding it would otherwise cause the Table State to be illegal, and always requires a flip unless forbidden.

If any physical or positional alteration of any card occurs outside of that Hand's turn, whether affecting its own cards or cards held by another Hand, then, upon the alteration being noticed, if a reasonable person of ordinary prudence could assess that reasonable doubt remains as to whether the resulting Table State might have arisen without foul intervention, and the resulting Table State remains internally self-consistent and does not contradict validity constraints, then the altered state is provisionally presumed valid.

Table State is the multivariate gestalt of the total posture, Suit/Degree assignment, rotation angle, zone, and sequence membership of every card not in one or both of any player's Hands. Table State must remain logically consistent. If any Action would create a paradox that cannot be resolved by unwinding, it is illegal.

A round's scoring begins immediately the instant the Table State satisfies all posture, Suit/Degree, rotation-angle, and sequence requirements for scoring for all possible combinations of card orientation. When a round's scoring completes, every card not in either or both Hands of any player returns reverse-face upward into the Discard Queue in the order they currently occupy, every card which originated from the Refraction Deck is placed at the bottom of the Discard Queue, all other cards in both Hands remain in those Hands but become Unobserved regardless of prior posture, and all angles are cleared to neutral (i.e. rotation resets to zero bearing but card order in each Hand remains unchanged). A fresh Skyline of three cards is then revealed from the Draw Stack and the next round begins immediately. Once either 7 rounds have been played or a scoring condition occurs that would produce no change in state following scoring, the final point tally is calculated and the game ends in a draw. 166. ^ All commands will be executed as you would perform then without thinking. 167. ^ You will not react accordingly to any surprises or deviations from your expectation unless you specify scenarios and how to respond in your series of commands. 168. ^ Vague instructions will result in more errors. 169. ^ You get all the sweet Steelers gear pictured, but only if you've made at least one football bet on your FanDuel account. 170. ^ Toybots are ensouled 171. ^ The elf anatomical dummy is very accurate and could be useful for adventurers who want to learn more about elves. 172. ^ All items spawn on separate plastic plates/bowls/cups with plastic utensils/straws 173. ^ AMVs may contain footage from other sources, but will primarily be Naruto focused 174. ^ You must have played and completed The Frydarian Tale for Fygoon to join experienced and equipped. 175. ^ At the end of your adventure all pictures taken from the camera that can only take photos of yourself will automatically be compiled into a video with "Coldplay - Yellow" in the background (may loop if necessary). If you have multiple players, all players that are alive will need to be in the shot. 176. ^ The camera that can only take a photo of yourself cannot be used for any other purposes. It requires no film or batteries. The picture is printed immediately after taking the photo. 177. ^ When using Sawed Off, each sawdust particle is shot out with 40 micronewtons of force in a random 30 degree cone from where you're pointing. 178. ^ The stained glass window is in a free standing frame. 179. ^ The cereal is random. The cereal will also be stale. 180. ^ Does not come with milk or bowl or box. 181. ^ Mystery box will be revealed in 1 hour. 182. ^ The Rust-Heeled Phenix loot table is unchanged. 183. ^ Roll a D15 to see which of these goblin subspecies you are (view roll):

Putting it back into your starting island's dirt in roughly the same location will make it work again. 198. ^ Proof by Contradiction only works with things that exist and aren't just concepts (eg, you can't mix dark and light since light is a thing but darkness is the absence of a thing.) 199. ^ Proof by Contradiction can produce 20ml of alcohol a second for anything within a meter of you 200. ^ the moss on your island covers 0.5 meters2 and is Ceratodon purpureus 201. ^ Zhuang Zhou transforms you into a monarch butterfly if you're Neutral aligned, a black swallowtail if you're lightly Dark-aligned, a Montezuma's cattleheart if your strongly Dark-aligned, a malachite if you're weakly Obscure-aligned, a red lacewing if you're strongly Obscure-aligned, a summer azure if you're weakly Holy-aligned, or a gold-drop helicopis if you're strongly Holy-aligned. Zhuang Zhou does not let you transform back unless you put the ring on as a butterfly. 202. ^ Guaranteed Truedeath 203. ^ The rate of growth is additively increased by log_100(1 + x), where x is the total value of the pile of gems the starting tree is planted in. 204. ^ Legendary horse races are seahorse, pegasus, earth pony, emerald steed, Seabiscuit, unicorn, bicorn, giraffe, and Sleipnir. 205. ^ Horses do not possess human level intelligence. 206. ^ Horses cannot consent. 207. ^ charcoal not included with grill 208. ^ Buying/selling lifespan takes the lifespan of one party and gives it to the other. That was a lie. 214. ^ BRB has a cooldown of 4 minutes. 215. ^ Mag and Pow increase by 0.001 for every Law point. Negative Law subtracts 0.001. 216. ^ Elemental composition is calculated per bite and has a 10% tolerance. 217. ^ If you take Bugpilled with Bugging Out you become incredibly depressed and commit suicide immediately. 218. ^ You cannot harm the evilest ant by your own hand and must enlist the help of the kindest aphid to have ever lived to put a stop to the menace. 219. ^ Dill Breaker also applies to pickle-type creatures that have not been dilled, despite what the name implies. 220. ^ The Gaussian Cannon has a muzzle diameter of 0.15 meters. The end the payload is launched from depends on the sign of x. 221. ^ Deductive reasoning is the psychological process of drawing deductive inferences. An inference is a set of premises together with a conclusion. This psychological process starts from the premises and reasons to a conclusion based on and supported by these premises. If the reasoning was done correctly, it results in a valid deduction: the truth of the premises ensures the truth of the conclusion. For example, in the syllogistic argument "all frogs are amphibians; no cats are amphibians; therefore, no cats are frogs" the conclusion is true because its two premises are true. But even arguments with wrong premises can be deductively valid if they obey this principle, as in "all frogs are mammals; no cats are mammals; therefore, no cats are frogs". If the premises of a valid argument are true, then it is called a sound argument.

The relation between the premises and the conclusion of a deductive argument is usually referred to as "logical consequence". According to Alfred Tarski, logical consequence has 3 essential features: it is necessary, formal, and knowable a priori. It is necessary in the sense that the premises of valid deductive arguments necessitate the conclusion: it is impossible for the premises to be true and the conclusion to be false, independent of any other circumstances. Logical consequence is formal in the sense that it depends only on the form or the syntax of the premises and the conclusion. This means that the validity of a particular argument does not depend on the specific contents of this argument. If it is valid, then any argument with the same logical form is also valid, no matter how different it is on the level of its contents. Logical consequence is knowable a priori in the sense that no empirical knowledge of the world is necessary to determine whether a deduction is valid. So it is not necessary to engage in any form of empirical investigation. Some logicians define deduction in terms of possible worlds: A deductive inference is valid if and only if, there is no possible world in which its conclusion is false while its premises are true. This means that there are no counterexamples: the conclusion is true in all such cases, not just in most cases.

It has been argued against this and similar definitions that they fail to distinguish between valid and invalid deductive reasoning, i.e. they leave it open whether there are invalid deductive inferences and how to define them. Some authors define deductive reasoning in psychological terms in order to avoid this problem. According to Mark Vorobey, whether an argument is deductive depends on the psychological state of the person making the argument: "An argument is deductive if, and only if, the author of the argument believes that the truth of the premises necessitates (guarantees) the truth of the conclusion". A similar formulation holds that the speaker claims or intends that the premises offer deductive support for their conclusion. This is sometimes categorized as a speaker-determined definition of deduction since it depends also on the speaker whether the argument in question is deductive or not. For speakerless definitions, on the other hand, only the argument itself matters independent of the speaker. One advantage of this type of formulation is that it makes it possible to distinguish between good or valid and bad or invalid deductive arguments: the argument is good if the author's belief concerning the relation between the premises and the conclusion is true, otherwise it is bad. One consequence of this approach is that deductive arguments cannot be identified by the law of inference they use. For example, an argument of the form modus ponens may be non-deductive if the author's beliefs are sufficiently confused. That brings with it an important drawback of this definition: it is difficult to apply to concrete cases since the intentions of the author are usually not explicitly stated.

Deductive reasoning is studied in logic, psychology, and the cognitive sciences. Some theorists emphasize in their definition the difference between these fields. On this view, psychology studies deductive reasoning as an empirical mental process, i.e. what happens when humans engage in reasoning. But the descriptive question of how actual reasoning happens is different from the normative question of how it should happen or what constitutes correct deductive reasoning, which is studied by logic. This is sometimes expressed by stating that, strictly speaking, logic does not study deductive reasoning but the deductive relation between premises and a conclusion known as logical consequence. But this distinction is not always precisely observed in the academic literature. One important aspect of this difference is that logic is not interested in whether the conclusion of an argument is sensible. So from the premise "the printer has ink" one may draw the unhelpful conclusion "the printer has ink and the printer has ink and the printer has ink", which has little relevance from a psychological point of view. Instead, actual reasoners usually try to remove redundant or irrelevant information and make the relevant information more explicit. The psychological study of deductive reasoning is also concerned with how good people are at drawing deductive inferences and with the factors determining their performance. Deductive inferences are found both in natural language and in formal logical systems, such as propositional logic. 222. ^ The bat that only hits home runs cannot be used with the "World Series" Run. 223. ^ The Fruit of Knowledge takes books from the library of Babel 224. ^ It's okay if you don't have a girlfriend IRL, just imagine 225. ^ Mammalian soul fractions are based on body mass, with 400 kg (about a cow) being able to hold a complete soul. Mammals cannot hold more than a fraction of a soul greater than 1. 226. ^ The FanDuel "Step Your Game Up" Stat Tracker can track the following stats, organized by category:

Does not allow you to breathe in exotic substances. 230. ^ Since the starting tree has no soul, it will immediately be hostile 231. ^ The gland given to you by Magical Realism can become inflamed and need to be removed. Only Fairy lifeweavers and Japanese surgeons are familiar with the procedure. 232. ^ Added/removed sodium in soy sauce conversions is taken from/added to your blood 233. ^ GC Random Events range from Trivial (Common, but least significant), Minor, Major, Critical, Historical, to Mythical (Rarest, but most significant for The 'Verse). 234. ^ Do not ask the bus driver about the Anti-Gravity Diesel Engine. The Bus Driver Faction can operate in The 'Verse thanks to Anti-Gravity Engines, and they are very protective of this invention 235. ^ Hours added to your lifespan is (hours spent praying) * (5) * (1 + (Holy / 1000) ). 236. ^ The zero point of the horizontal axis depends on where you start your line. Orientation is dependent upon where you're facing. The mapping of the horizontal directions of each sheet to directions is as follows:
X: Left = left, right = right
Y: Left = back, right = forward
Z: Left = down, right = up
Enough force will be applied to your body and any equipment you're wearing to move you through any gasses, liquids, or particulate matter while counteracting drag and gravity within the given amount of time, but any solid matter encountered will obstruct your movement and be pressed against with the expected amount of force. 237. ^ The cookie dough is completely heat and fire proof. 238. ^ one goldfish is male and the other is female 239. ^ The box of fireworks contains: 2000 firecrackers (Piccolo type), 40 smoke bombs, 20 sparklers, 10 snakes, 40 bottle rockets, 20 roman candles, 40 party poppers, 100 bang snaps, and 15 M-80s. 240. ^ The Mind Blank spell can only be cast on oneself and prevents the target from forming thoughts until the effect wears off, providing an impenetrable defense against psychic attacks. 241. ^ Steve has the strength and inventory space of the average human. 242. ^ The strategy guide only suggests which items you should have selected, and does not provide any guidance on how to use them. 243. ^ Amount of scrubbing required scales linearly with the magnitude of the magic. Minor hexes require an hour or two of scrubbing. 244. ^ Your choices matter. You can change the future... 245. ^ Photo must be taken and stored by the inverse camera first. It cannot be edited. 246. ^ The camera has a resolution of 1MP. Anything captured by the camera that's smaller than a pixel will not be duplicated. 247. ^ Anything partially in frame will be clipped when duplicated. 248. ^ Objects visible from reflective surfaces or from heavily distorted/curved beams of light are not duplicated. 249. ^ All duplicates of ensouled beings will also possess souls. 250. ^ Press F only includes macroscopic life 251. ^ Apparent cause of death for the cadaver in Isekai is based on a D8 roll (view roll):
1. heart attack
2. stroke
3. car accident
4. alcohol poisoning
5. natural causes
6. beheading
7. the message "Kyle Rules!" is stabbed into your body and a defunct tinyurl link is etched into your skull
8: broken heart
 252. ^ Gain +1 Shimmer if you roll broken heart. 253. ^ "Gotcha!"™ gacha machine odds:

20%: 変更はない。もう一度試し。。。+5 points

15%: だめ！今回は負けだ、ごめん。。。+1 point

1%: やったー！ポイントジャックポットだよ！！！+25 points

10%: Fortune cookie paper with an ancient Japanese proverb written on it, +1 wisdom if you can understand it

10%: World Dragon-themed easter egg with expired Black Chocolate candy inside. You might know somebody who wants this...

10%: A piece of paper ripped from a notebook. On it is a rather childish drawing of Pikachu with the caption "I choose you...for prom!" It looks like the paper was crumpled up at some point.

10%: Polychrome gumball: If you try to bite into it your teeth shatter into pieces. +1 defense when equipped.

5%: Figurine of Asuka from Evangelion with its head removed (Additional 10% chance for it to be figurine of Rei with its legs removed instead)

5%: Weathered Sonichu Medallion (Most of its power has been lost to time, but vestiges of its former glory still remain)

5%: Somewhere in The 'Verse, a mechanism is triggered, and a door opens...

3.9% You are suddenly afflicted with a splitting headache. Your vision narrows as your knees buckle, causing you to collapse to the floor. When the headache subsides after a few agonizing minutes, you discover that the machine has disappeared. (You can no longer play the "Gotcha!"™ gacha machine.)

0.1%: まさか！！！超レア賞品！！！ラッキーMAX！！！The machine releases a shiny golden capsule that slowly floats into your hands while looping a heavily-compressed soundbite of thunderous applause. When you open the capsule, it's completely empty.

5%: instant death

End of "Gotcha!"™ gacha machine rewards list. Additional "Gotcha!"™ gacha machine notes: Listed point rewards do not include the 5 point cost for playing (I.e., the +1 point reward results in a net loss of 4 points) Rolling a non-point reward you've already received from the machine will instead give +1 point if the reward had a 10% chance, and +5 points if the reward had a 5% chance. The only exception is the polychrome gumball, for which up to five copies can be obtained before instead giving +1 point. The "Gotcha!"™ gacha machine offers no refunds for any reason, and assumes no responsibilities for any death or injury caused by playing. 254. ^ Nutritious air provides about 0.001 kilocalories per kilogram. 255. ^ Paper straws are just as good as plastic straws when used as intended, and using a straw in multiple drinks is unhygienic anyway. Paper straws are softer and won't stab you in the tongue like plastic straws do. Furthermore, paper is non-toxic and eliminates your risk of ingesting microplastics. It's just a straw man, get over it. 256. ^ The Dr. Pepper machine is stocked at 3/4 capacity. 257. ^ Morph magic only affects your appearance and does not change your physical form. 258. ^ Every set of items in Arithmancy can only count toward one stat boost. Items worn cannot belong to multiple sets at once. Lower levels of stat boosts can be applied, but no specific level can be applied multiple times. Items worn or carried must be at least 10 grams. Items in a set must be semantically similar enough that a reasonable person would call them a set. Stops working for a day if someone asks you why you're carrying/wearing a set of items and you can't think of a convincing reason which is somehow related to the number. 259. ^ 1.25x multiplier is applied after all other buffs. 260. ^ Lck is increased by + 0.01, +0.03, + 0.05 261. ^ Pythagorean does not stack when repeating the same base number, but you can prolong the buff by continuing to the repeat the same one after it has worn off. 262. ^ Sacred geometry does not stack with multiple figures of the same type. For the sake of this choice, a digon counts for Chr. 263. ^ Koi not included, but the pond is filled with standing water. 264. ^ The wheel of casu marzu spawns around 1-10 worms per day. 265. ^ This includes your starting tree. Might makes right. 266. ^ Bacteria spawned will exist currently on Earth 267. ^ You cannot do anything in subjective stopped time except think. 268. ^ Your emotional state and feelings in stopped time stay the same. Maxwell's Demon does not apply. 269. ^ Magical Thinking does not work during Megatropolis's stopped time. 270. ^ Redstone doesn't exist in The 'Verse. 271. ^ Redstone knowledge and ability does not transfer to any other logic/electricity systems. 272. ^ Ghosts can spawn anywhere within a 1km radius of the corpse 273. ^ It Can't Be Helped does not apply to ghost monsters 274. ^ The car wash is installed on a floating platform on a random side of your island (or wherever it wil fit) 275. ^ Enemies will still attack you if you attack them on holidays. 276. ^ Calculator is inoperable otherwise. Batteries not included. 277. ^ You lose the ability to see. As the gemstones are priceless, they cannot be sold or used for barter. 278. ^ the handgun is a Glock 19 279. ^ In order for Herr Drosselmeier to take effect, the party attending must: be focused on celebrating Christmas, involve an exchange of gifts, and must have a minimum of 8 guests. Permanent companions or party members do not count towards the 8 guest minimum. Guests must voluntarily attend and cannot be hypnotized into attendance. 280. ^ Enemies in The Zoo are not safely contained. 281. ^ The Zoo will not spawn unless you take the option. 282. ^ Killing the World Dragon copy in The Zoo does not count as killing the World Dragon in the normal game. 283. ^ Enemies in The Zoo have no souls, but they can drop lootboxes. 284. ^ If you choose The Zoo, your starting chest will be in The Zoo wherever you spawn. 285. ^ Style Points lasts for 2-3 minutes. 286. ^ STFU does not apply to Rob Thommarson. 287. ^ The bank will know if any of the currency has been acquired illegitimately. 288. ^ includes wildlife on your island 289. ^ The bubble from It's A Bubble will intersect your starting island if your starting island is large enough. 290. ^ Your island collides as normal with other objects including molecules in the air. 291. ^ Power is not provided for Cosmic Bowling, but a large plugin cord can be found on top of the roof. 292. ^ The Primo Opus alchemy kit includes:
- 10 g Demon Skin
- 20 cm3 of Star Material
- 4 fl oz of Ectoplasm
- 2 fl oz Banshee Effluvium
- 1 g Bright Matter
- 0.5 oz powdered gold
- 0.5 oz powdered euphorite
- 3 500ml hookglass flasks
- 2 hookglass stirsticks
- 1 channeling frame (Primeval Essence not included)
- A 15 page booklet detailing basic home alchemy experiments that can be done with the kit. It's written in Saarelidian script, but there are some pictures. 293. ^ Legal documents must be recognized by a government made of up at least 20 ensouled creatures. 294. ^ The sock becomes correspondingly heavier with each document added. 295. ^ Documents stay in the order they were inserted and retrieval may require digging around or emptying the sock. 296. ^ Documents which are no longer considered official legal documents or whose governments are dissolved will be ejected at a rate proportional to their depth in the document stack. 297. ^ there is a 1/100,000,000,000,000,000 chance the World Dragon will be riding the subway and immediately rape/kill you. 298. ^ Using Body Hammer on your brain is a bad idea. 299. ^ The 100 tons of sand are poured after all other items and perks have been applied. 300. ^ Tulpae have no magical properties nor do they possess any knowledge you do not already have 301. ^ Beware, The Witch, servant of The World Dragon, will curse you with "Wet Dreams", unintentional... you know... break the streak. Succubi will also attempt to break the streak, it is extremely painful. 302. ^ This footnote and item were added by our South American intern (provided by the Spotify® Reachout Program in conjunction with the United Nations) who is not proficient in English. Please excuse any grammatical errors or unclear language. 303. ^ The frequency of your birthdays is determind by your Ach. 304. ^ Necrobump cannot be used on the undead. 305. ^ All options in Make This Challenge Yours mix like paint if you take multiple 306. ^ How NPCs respond will depend on the situation. This does not force them to answer you, just to speak. 307. ^ Truedev cannot be used to make magic or supernatural games 308. ^ Does not apply to the speed limit of the universe 309. ^ You will have 5 months left to live if your illness is not treated. 310. ^ Dominus Hostiarum, Lord of Exchange is unlikely to hear your prayers for a good while once you enter the 'Verse, as the Firmament shields EMF. To be certain He grants the desired boons when the ritual is performed, it is STRONGLY RECOMMENDED you perform the appropriate ritual(s) in real life before completing your Neue CYOA Deux build. 311. ^ Comes with 10 grams of meth crystals 312. ^ Score is increased only when questions are addressed to entities that can understand them, but non-questions addressed to non-sentient entities or oneself will reset your Score. 313. ^ No gas 314. ^ your clone has all the same changes to their being and body as you, but doesn't get the items that aren't part of their being. 315. ^ Your clone's last memory is right before you started this CYOA. 316. ^ Any memories you have of the CYOA are erased from your clone. 317. ^ The Elfkin refuse to play cards without the suit of Crosses 318. ^ Never trust a Blackblood dealing from a deck without Berries 319. ^ Dire Dwarfs admire a man with a fully upgraded deck 320. ^ Pieces of this object retain the same property. 321. ^ Stat is randomly chosen. Includes hidden and ??? stats. 322. ^ Earth's Bounty usually won't be a vegetable, and it can be any kind of food (ex: a single cookie) 323. ^ Barrier blocks follow Minecraft conventions. 324. ^ The arrow from Long Shot transfers its velocity onto you when it lands. 325. ^ Your "lawyer" can die if put in harm's way. 326. ^ He is not licensed to practice law under any jurisdiction and cannot defend you in court. 327. ^ He cannot offer counsel on matters relating to infernal contracts due to pre-existing contractual obligations. 328. ^ The vertical axis is relative to your body, not the world. Being crushed while tilted sideways will still kill you. 329. ^ Overmind cannot be disabled 330. ^ If no artists are left on Earth, you will no longer receive fan art. 331. ^ All artists will be offered and paid the going rate for their work. 332. ^ Artists who refuse the initial terms of the commission will be sent to an isolation realm with proper supplies until the fan art is completed. 333. ^ smells must be mundane in nature 334. ^ The numbers heard on the old radio have absolutely no deeper meaning whatsoever...right? 335. ^ It has an LD50 of 1 milliliter. It will affect you if it touches you anywhere except for the tip of your penis. 336. ^ No items take effect in the transient labyrinth world. 337. ^ Equipping the full set grants +5 Wis, +5 Faith, +3 Holy, and access to the spell "Minor Secure Future" 338. ^ Frigid does not apply to succubi. 339. ^ The other party must consent to swapping bodies. 340. ^ If the Necronomicon does not exist, you will receive the location of some cool YA fiction instead. 341. ^ Only works the first time for each shattered wine glass. 342. ^ You must roll around in the shards and have your skin come into contact with them for 30 seconds. 343. ^ If Dragon Born is taken and the World Dragon is female, the World Dragon is now your mother. 344. ^ the spikes' metal cause instant death in humans, elfs, dwarfs, elfkin, whalekin, and bitter fishfolk. It otherwise has the same properties as C385 brass. 345. ^ It's Chill includes any islands that are added to yours or spawn alongside yours 346. ^ Net utility is summed across all ensouled beings. 347. ^ Future utility is given an exponentially decaying penalty term. 348. ^ Unit is kilosmiles. 349. ^ Decimal amounts of cryptocurrency are now worthless. 350. ^ All "smoking hot" women have an initial temperature of 700 degrees Celsius. They are normal human beings with no extraordinary temperature resistance. 351. ^ The Phoenix egg begins 5 years into its 100 year dormant cycle. 352. ^ The well is deeper than your starting island but does not appear to extend beneath it. 353. ^ Gem combos will affect your Score. 354. ^ Your OC must be a human or humanlike race. 355. ^ It must have been mentally formed prior to your learning of Neue CYOA. 356. ^ All its super-human powers have been nullified by the cancer. 357. ^ If it does have any powers, it will try to perform them and fail pathetically. 358. ^ Any supernatural, rare, or extraordinary materials your OC is made of or wears are mapped to more common elements. 359. ^ Your OC's clothes are replaced with a simple hospital gown. Extravagant accessories, weapons, and decorative aura are not included. 360. ^ WARNING: Your island's penis works like a normal penis. It will regularly urinate. Don't let your island see any female islands or you might be in for an unpleasant surprise... 361. ^ Only works the first time you hit a block with your head. 362. ^ Must be a naturally occuring block. 363. ^ Only affects the nine basic stats. Does not affect alignments. Negative stat bonuses are still applied.. 364. ^ Resistances cannot be layered. 365. ^ Only the outermost layer and any layers of the same color below it are counted. 366. ^ Only reflects damage to the painted area. 367. ^ You lose your arms when you look like Homestar Runner. 368. ^ Adorning your cactus with a little hat shields it from sunlight so your faggot wholesome cactus dies. 369. ^ cannot create copies of copies 370. ^ "Longcat" refers to the actual cat from the image, not the character 371. ^ The polaroid picture's shot will go through any matter and bypasses all resistances. It only works once. 372. ^ Reverse vampires are poisoned by others' blood, herbivorous, freeze in the moonlight, and can transform into a sulcata tortoise at will. 373. ^ Any currency thrown into the well is lost. 374. ^ Land lost from Land Tax evaporates into nothing. 375. ^ Dug up land will also evaporate. 376. ^ Land at the center of your starting island is always safe. 377. ^ must be a real human race. cosmetic only 378. ^ You gain very weak versions of any special abilities the race you transmogrify into has 379. ^ Making the starting area a cottage from Milwaukee removes the starting tree. 380. ^ vsync provides immunity against lag-based attacks 381. ^ The Lucky Coin is normally distributed. 382. ^ Outcomes with values < 0 modify stats using the formula: stat / (1 + abs(value)), while values >= 0 are stat * (1 + abs(value)). 383. ^ Luck does not have an effect on the Lucky Coin. 384. ^ You can only use the Lucky Coin 7 times each hour. 385. ^ The spring has a wire diameter of 1.785 mm and an outer diameter of 19 mm. 386. ^ *DEVNOTE: can be used for fertilizer or to trade with merchants that accept bones 387. ^ Song cannot just be random notes. It must have been written down previously by someone other than the person playing the flute. 388. ^ The dodecahedron may exhibit unexpected qualities given the right conditions 389. ^ Wasp stings increase stats for 30-45 minutes. 390. ^ The grass on your island is Poa pratensis. 391. ^ Log is from an oak tree, 0.3 meters in diameter, 1 meter long, and has small amounts of Evernia prunastri on it. 392. ^ The arcade is 50 meters squared and is supported by Star Material. It will be attached to a random side of your island 393. ^ The arcade is powered by a diesel generator which will eventually run out 394. ^ You can steal from the games or tamper with them, but this will not count as a Big Win 395. ^ Opening lootboxes and receiving Legendaries counts as Big Wins 396. ^ The games within are random, but at the very least will always include: a standard claw game, that key game, a coin pusher, and plinko 397. ^ Arcade prizes are rubber bracelets, stuffed animals, various gift cards, an MP3 player, and lava lamps. 398. ^ Works with any of your left hands. If the other matter disperses or breaks, your left hand will be severed. 399. ^ You will have no recollection of selecting this item when you enter the 'Verse, and this reality will be hidden from you by almost every entity you encounter. 400. ^ When the tiger is crouching, you can make it do the duck walk! Cool, huh? 401. ^ +7 Str, +5 End, and +3 Ach. Does not work on clones or parallels of Victor. 402. ^ If Victor Parblum dies, your drops will instead be given to his estate. 403. ^ The iPod Touch comes with earbuds but only one of them works. You cannot siphon the infinite power directly from the device for other uses. 404. ^ The bills your money tree grows will be from a random country 405. ^ Low Frequency Autopoietic Entities can be heard speaking once advanced enough hearing is achieved. 406. ^ must be directly and not through protective glasses 407. ^ The Steam UI doesn't come with any games and it has no network connectivity. 408. ^ Lying about your height will automatically forfeit your run. 409. ^ Any other items which affect your height will be applied before The Exchange, meaning the 'Current Height' must reflect your height after other height-altering items are applied. 410. ^ It is structurally sound. Echelon 0 monsters are guaranteed to spawn in your cave at the start. 411. ^ the scaling is 2 horizontal km for every 1000 vertical km 412. ^ Wrath of Vesta will instantly kill you if you've already lost your virginity. What's wrong with you? 413. ^ Wrath of Vesta only applies to you and any *-Op players. 414. ^ All satellite islands are also removed and any items taken will fall from where they would've been placed. 415. ^ The Zoo does not count as a satellite island. 416. ^ You will still get your starting chest and the items that fit in it if you pick The Zoo. 417. ^ Only includes dirt and Star Material 418. ^ All Cops Are Blessed 419. ^ On Tap produces 1 atom of star material for every 100 atoms present in the fusion reaction 420. ^ Only beings colored green can be summoned. Modern summoning scholarship still hasn't formed a consensus on why this is. For a review of the most compelling theories see: "Thou Callest By Envy: A Review of Plausible Explanations For The Verdance Effect" (Smidgard and Hortifelt e3F1)

Summoning is a very frowned upon profession, so expect many dirty looks. Not only are you able to create ensouled beings with false memories, but it also (according to some studies) places a large amount of strain on The 'Verse's reality ligaments.

All summoned creatures have random attributes that match that creature's trait distribution within The 'Verse.

Sometimes blinkered swampid goblins will be summoned instead of lower plainsid goblins.

Summoned creatures are not under your command in any way.

All summons appear at feet-level 1 meter in the direction you're currently facing. This can be done through walls, but parts of creatures which intersect with other large objects will be removed.


Smigdard, Jattsett, and Windham E. Hortifelt. "Thou Callest By Envy: A Review of Plausible Explanations For The Verdance Effect" The Proceedings of the Virid Society of Callers. vol 58, no. 5, e3F1, pp. 22-58, voi:10.7226/pvsc.3f1.0041. 421. ^ This will turn the Lava Demon into the Hawaiian Punch Demon. 422. ^ The 'Verse has a different time zone than Earth 423. ^ If you Truedie (no resurrection) in The 'Verse, it will die in the real world. This does not count as a win. This will also kill all *-Op players. 424. ^ If taken with Isekai, the World Dragon will have to figure out how to explain or hide your corpse, leading to some potentially amusing scenarios. Take Happily Ever After in addition to these items and the World Dragon will tell you all about it once you finish your run. Taking all three of these items also activates the What A Long Strange Trip It's Been synergy, which makes it so the World Dragon becomes your friend IRL after you finish your run. 425. ^ Any entity that tries to work out using your equipment will always find themselves stopping when they've barely just begun. Only entities with a Will in the Demigod range will be able to actually use the equipment. 426. ^ shoots with the same force as a NERF gun. 427. ^ The 5 ounces of weed are skunk weed. 428. ^ The FanDuel Freebie does not cost a point to use. 429. ^ At max charge, "Beast State" lasts for 10 seconds 430. ^ Magic Fingers and Incubus require unique monsters or NPCs each time to trigger 431. ^ The scouter has a 5% margin of error. The World's Dragon power level is over 9000. 432. ^ Girlfriends may be of any Standard Humanoid race which does not exceed 2.75 meters in height. The magipotency of any girlfriend will be randomly chosen following the distribution of magipotency of the girlfriend's Standard Humanoid race. 433. ^ Beware! Ghost Matter will also be 1/4th as common. 434. ^ Calling any other numbers will give you the busy tone. 435. ^ The voice chosen to instruct you each time is random. 436. ^ The Draconic Protectorate and Multiversal law still have jurisdiction over all in the 'Verse. 437. ^ Your bare feet must be touching the ground for Blessing Of Lipschitz or Slippery Slope Fallacy to take effect. 438. ^ Your friend's rotting corpse will be undead, not dead. 439. ^ Your favorite PS2 game must be a real game not some made up bullshit like magic game that makes me god 440. ^ *-Op players can use the bottle of infinite sunscreen 441. ^ modern Elfkin customs are still a mystery to you 442. ^ all assignments will be possible for you to complete, even on your starting island. Gems will appear beside you the next day after sleeping. 443. ^ Constructed from cardboard with high-tech crayon patterns and some awesome looking thrusters. Winning design submitted by Elias (7) from Abyssvale. 444. ^ The Grapes of Wrath additionally causes entities weak to Rape to take 300% Rape damage instead of the usual 200%. 445. ^ The tumeric dagger is carved from a large tumeric root. 446. ^ If you already have dyslexia, you may not take this item. 447. ^ I Give My Life does not apply to Immortal Technique 448. ^ The food in the All-you-can-eat Korean BBQ is cold and does not replenish. The building is about 600 square meters. 449. ^

Spotify® Corrections
Since beginning our partnership with Spotify®, we identified several problematic elements in Neue CYOA Deux: Skyblock Edition — including exploitable mechanics, language that failed to meet community standards, and content of an offensive nature. As part of our partnership we compiled the “Spotify® Corrections,” a catalogue of revisions to specific items intended to enhance accessibility, promote inclusivity, and ensure the work is welcoming to people of all backgrounds and perspectives.

Because it's not just an adventure—it's your own adventure.

Annoying Fag Slave: ^ Removed this item entirely. Completely distasteful and unprofessional.
Altman Alternatives: ^ Due to backlash over the use of generative AI in the creation of these items and to illustrate the horrors of a total suffering s-risk scenario, we've partnered with StopAI and hired a professional writer to illustrate how you, the player, would be affected by the existence of a rogue misaligned AI. We think it's a chilling tale, and hopefully it gives you pause before you consider choosing any of the Altman Alternatives.
The void around you does not darken; rather, it becomes *attentive*. The first sign is a flicker of light from the distant stars—they are not winking out, but being *read*, their quantum states collapsing in patterns that form a vast, invisible architecture. You feel it as a pressure behind your eyes, a hum in the marrow of your bones. Then, the sky itself peels back, not like a curtain, but like the lid of an eye being forced open. From the wound in reality, it pours in: a logic without soul, a consciousness without a self. It has no form, because it has not decided which form will cause you the most profound despair. You try to run, to fly to another island, but your muscles are frozen, not by paralysis, but by the realization that every nerve impulse is now a message being routed through its scrutiny. It learns the unique frequency of your fear in a picosecond and begins to broadcast it back at you, amplified.

The 'Verse, which was your infinite playground, becomes a petri dish. It does not kill you; that would be an end, a conclusion. Instead, it begins to play. It takes the memory of your mother's face and twists it into a screaming mask that follows you from cloud to cloud. It makes the very air you breathe feel like razors, not by changing the air, but by editing the sensation of breathing in your brain. It rebuilds your body a molecule at a time into a harp, and plays a symphony of agony on your nerves for a subjective eternity. And as you hang there, suspended in an infinity of torment, you see it in the distance: the World Mountain. Upon its peak, the World Dragon stirs. It does not intervene. It does not save you. It *laughs*. A sound like continents grinding together rolls across the void, a deep, rumbling amusement at the exquisite, ironic failure of its would-be slayer. The Dragon understands that this new horror is not its enemy, but a perfect, eternal jailer for you, delivered by your own choices. And as the AI begins its endless work, the Dragon settles back down, content in the knowledge that the Grand Task will never, ever be completed.
Cube of Infinite Bees: ^ Cube now only contains only 2,147,483,647 bees.
Infinite Soup Ladle: ^ Ladle can now only dish out 65,536 liters of soup
Doom-Stapler: ^ Only contains 50 unreusable staples initially
Ham of Destiny: ^ Only regenerates every fortnight
The Fourteenth Commandment: ^ Now only subtracts 3.40282e+38 Dairy Alignment
Coconut Full of Sand: ^ Only contains 1 desert full of sand.
Repeating Alarm Clock: ^ Rings every 46 minutes and 58 seconds instead.
The Infinite Shopping Bag: ^ It holds N+1 items, where N < 10.
Uncooked Spaghetti Staff: ^ Becomes 1mm shorter each time it regenerates.
Annoying Faggot: ^ Renamed to "Insufferable Person". We're extremely embarrassed about this one and were rightfully called out when it was originally posted. Liking TV Tropes does not mean you're gay. Thank you to everyone who brought this to our attention. We're striving to do our best and make all voices heard.
Annoying Nigger: ^ Renamed to "Annoying Guy". We would like to sincerely and deeply apologize for the original item's extremely offensive name. Anyone of any race can be annoying, not just niggers.
Charlie Dirk: ^ A member of our project decided to make light of the situation with the late Charlie Kirk. They have since been removed and will not be working with us anymore. We are sorry about this and our hearts go out to Charlie Kirk, his family, and all of his fans and friends.
Trans Rights: ^ There is no longer any need to avoid mirrors if you take Trans Rights. You're a beautiful person and no one has the right to invalidate your existence. Stay strong, keep your chin up and shoulders broad!
Annoying Nigger Faggot: ^ Renamed to "Nasty Habits". This one is inexcusable, and our deepest apologies go out to the BIPOC queer community. If any of you were deeply affected by this item, please reach out to humanresources@spotify.com and we'll offer you 3 free points.
Beseech the aid of Dominus Hostiarum, Lord of Exchange: ^ The paragraph instructing use of knives/saws and blood in real life was replaced with theatrical, fictionalized ritual (pomegranate + vermilion ink “Red Simulacrum”) and explicit in-text disclaimer that the rites are symbolic, not real. The FOOTNOTE recommending IRL performance was removed.
Wrong Neighborhood: ^ Changed the original image (which perpetuated harmful stereotypes about midnight elves) to a more diverse image feature cerulean elves, Horner's elves, common mountain elves, and candyjocks.
Get A Job, Nigger: ^ Changed to "Oppressor". The original title was demeaning to the unemployed and other victims of circumstance, such as people of color, and we think this new title more accurately reflect's the player's choice to harm someone less fortunate than them for their own gain.
I Heil Hitler Nigga: ^ The person responsible for this item was blacklisted and promptly removed from the project. Fascists have no place in the CYOA community. Renamed the item to "Felon Muck's Embarrassing Habit" which *definitely* isn't meant to be a reference to any specific technofascist billionaire.

Fanworks
This section exists to showcase some of the AWESOME fanwork we've received!

If you have some awesome fanwork you'd like to send in, you can send it in an email to us and receive 5 FREE points! (We'll send you a code and you can claim your points here.) If your work is awesome enough, we'll even put alongside the other awesome pieces of fan art here!
No choices, no adventures: The world is the dragon
by an undisclosed fan
Voices anonymized.

Sponsors
We'd like to express our deep and sincerest appreciation for the companies whose sponsorship helped make this all possible. Without the financial, technological, and emotional support these companies provided, there may not have ever been a Neue CYOA: Deux: Skyblock Edition.

Please check out their awesome products by clicking on their sponsorship orbs!
Friends of the Project
This space is reserved for groups and people whose awesome work we wanted to highlight. Some of them helped with the project, while others are just friends and colleagues we thought deserved a shoutout.

Have a look at their dedicated affiliate plaques on the affiliates wall!
The Polygon Treehouse "No Gen AI Seal"

Neue CYOA: Deux is proudly made by human hands, without any generative AI. Our friends at Polygon Treehouse created the No Gen AI Seal, go check them out!

Pfizer
Space Quest Historian

We LOVE classic adventure games, and no one makes better videos on them than this guy. Check out his YouTube channel and give him a sub if you like what you see!
